signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Intervention Risk + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.78 — 275bp Fed-BoJ carry (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 1.0%) sustains the bid despite Japan's retail sales smashing forecasts (5.3% vs 2.3% exp). 160.00 remains the key level previously triggering BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk is acute as price holds above this threshold. Retail 84% short vs COT LF net short -115,033 (28.4% long) creates a massive institutional vs retail divergence. Institutional carry-chasing dominates until VIX > 25.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail Extreme
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -23,314 (specs fading hawkish bias) vs RBNZ hike priced for 8 Jul. Retail sentiment is at an extreme 93% long, directly opposing the institutional short position. If RBNZ delivers the priced hike, short-squeeze risk is elevated as crowded spec shorts and retail longs collide. NZD/USD at 0.5644 remains under pressure from a +150bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25%).

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Vol Premium
signals.text: USD/CAD vs WTI Oil correlation at -0.743 vs 252d norm of -0.027 (z=-1.96σ) — the traditional CAD/Oil link is broken as macro drivers decouple. USD/CAD at 1.4188 is supported by a +150bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25%). Options market is pricing elevated protection with IV at 30.8% vs HV30 at 6.2% (VRP +24.6%). Retail 92% short suggests a contrarian extension higher if data continues to support USD.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Stance + Options Skew
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6893 — RBA's Kent signals less tolerance for inflation, supporting the 4.35% policy rate. Despite a -60bp carry deficit vs USD (Fed 3.75% vs RBA 4.35%), AUD/USD 25d RR at -0.98 confirms a moderate put skew as the options market pays for downside protection. High VRP of +15.2% (IV 22.7% vs HV30 7.5%) indicates significant hedging demand ahead of US NFP data.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + CESI Divergence
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1392 — IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.7% (VRP +2.4%) signals an elevated volatility premium as the market hedges against EUR weakness. CESI at -4 (net misses) supports a bearish EUR bias vs USD CESI at +291 (strong beats). The +150bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.25%) structurally limits EUR upside despite the ECB's hiking stance. Watch 1.1400 for technical resistance.
